heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 225 on 28 May 2026, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: Currently no stormy weather.  The most recent storm happened on 18 July 2026 and it lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: A significant dry period is on-going and is ranked 1st for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 22 consecutive days beginning 03 July 2026.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 3 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwave.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 28 June 2026, there have been only 68 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (206°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The largest wind run ever was on 21 October 2012 with a value of 1737 miles.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 9.2°C) and outside temperature (down 7.4°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 13%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 16697 lux) and cloud levels (up 1478 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 25667 lux) and cloud levels (up 2095 m) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 1478.0m lower than yesterday, 2100m lower than the past week, 1682.5m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 1490m to a maximum of 5530m, averaging 2110m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 4210m and 3790m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: Clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mild and comfortable temperatures. Outside; pleasant, mild temperatures. Also, humidity should not be a concern and very little to no wind will be present. Drizzle or light rain possible, clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight, and the light is absent. The weather will likely remain the same.

washing.phrase: It is nighttime; there will not be a great deal of drying overnight.
